title: Rule-Declare-Pages
description: JSON schema for creating or updating a Pega data page rule (Rule-Declare-Pages) via create-rule / update-rule. Only authored and identity fields are included; system-managed and versioning fields are omitted.

pyRuleName.description: Internal rule name — must match pyPageName exactly. Auto-derived; agents should omit.

pyPageName.description: Data page name. Convention: prefix with 'D_' (e.g., 'D_CustomerList', 'D_AccountTypes').

pyLabel.description: Human-readable display label shown in Dev Studio. Max length 64 characters.

pyDescription.description: Developer-facing description of what this data page provides.

pyClassName.description: Page class (for structure=page) or entry class (for structure=list). Must be a valid existing class with case-exact match. When pyStructure is 'list', this must be 'Code-Pega-List'.

pyStructure.description: Data page structure. 'page' = single record (clipboard Page). 'list' = collection (clipboard Page List).

pyScope.description: Cache scope. 'thread' = current flow execution (case-specific). 'requestor' = user session (user-specific lookups). 'node' = server node (shared reference data, code tables). When 'node', pyAccessGroup is required.

pyPageType.description: Authoritative user-facing page type selector. 'normal' = standard read-only data page (drives pyType='normal', pyIsSavable='false', pyUseNewGenPageForEditableDataPage='false'). 'savable' = supports save-back (drives pyType='loadonly', pyIsSavable='true'). 'loadonly' = DEPRECATED — do not use for read-only pages; use 'normal' instead. Legacy value retained for backward compatibility only.

pyType.description: Internal data page type. Derived from pyPageType: 'normal' → 'normal'; 'loadonly' → 'loadonly'; 'savable' → 'loadonly'. Agents should set pyPageType; pyType is derived.

pyEditable.description: Whether the data page contents are editable after load. Data pages were originally all read-only; leave blank (treated as 'false') unless editability is explicitly required.

pyRefreshStrategy.description: Refresh strategy. 'never' = cached until explicit flush or timeout. 'conditional' = refreshed based on conditions. 'reference' = refreshed on each reference.

pyRefreshOncePerInteraction.description: Whether to refresh each user interaction. Related to pyRefreshStrategy.

pyTimeoutDD.description: Timeout days component. Only meaningful when pyPageType is 'normal' and pyRefreshOncePerInteraction is 'false'. Leave blank when unused; the server persists the value regardless of whether the UI exposes it.

pyTimeoutHH.description: Timeout hours component (0–23). Only meaningful when pyPageType is 'normal' and pyRefreshOncePerInteraction is 'false'.

pyTimeoutMM.description: Timeout minutes component (0–59). Only meaningful when pyPageType is 'normal' and pyRefreshOncePerInteraction is 'false'.

pyTimeoutSS.description: Timeout seconds component (0–59). Only meaningful when pyPageType is 'normal' and pyRefreshOncePerInteraction is 'false'. When a timeout is configured, the sum of DD/HH/MM/SS must be > 0.

pyAsync.description: Whether to load the data page asynchronously.

pyAllowIdle.description: Allow the data page to enter idle state.

pyAllowDuplicates.description: Allow duplicate entries in list-type data pages.

pyFlushAll.description: Flush all cached instances on reload.

pyIsSavable.description: Whether the data page supports save-back to the source. Derived from pyPageType: 'savable' → 'true', otherwise 'false'. Agents should set pyPageType; pyIsSavable is derived.

pyUseNewGenPageForEditableDataPage.description: Controls whether the data page generates an editable copy for UI interaction. Set to 'false' for read-only data pages (pyPageType='normal'). Set to 'true' only for savable data pages that need UI-editable behavior. Derived — agents should not set directly; included for readback verification.

pyRefreshDataPageAfterSave.description: Whether the data page is automatically refreshed after a save operation.

pyIsSimulated.description: Whether this is a simulated data page — a runtime variant where the real source is replaced by a stand-in (e.g. LoadActivity with canned data). Simulated variants are selected via ruleset layering and are transparent to callers. Not related to unit-test mock pages.

pyIsQueryable.description: Whether the data page is backed by Pega Data Object storage. When 'true', no pyDataSourceList entry is needed.

pyUseWhen.description: Whether a When rule gates data page loading.

pyWhen.description: When rule name for conditional data page loading. Used with pyUseWhen: 'true'. Server clears this field when pyScope is 'node'.

pyIsClassFieldEditable.description: Whether the class field is editable.

pyEnableRetrievePageByKey.description: Enable key-based retrieval from a list data page. Server forces to 'false' when pyStructure is 'page' or pyType is 'loadonly'.

pyDisplayMode.description: Display mode. 'basic' = standard new rule. 'clone' = created via save-as (includes simulation variants). 'specialize' = specialization. 'advanced' = advanced mode.

pyAccessGroup.description: Access group restriction. Required when pyScope is 'node'. Server clears this field when pyScope is 'thread'.

pyRuleSet.description: Rule set the data page is saved to.

pyRuleSetVersion.description: Rule set version (e.g., '01-01-02'). Use '01-01-01' for branch authoring.

pyParameters.description: Data page parameters. Parameters become part of the cache key — different parameter values produce different cached instances.

pyDataSourceList.description: Data sources. At least one entry required for non-queryable data pages. Queryable data pages (pyIsQueryable: 'true') backed by Pega Data Object storage have zero sources. Multiple entries enable conditional source selection via pySourceWhen.

pyPagesAndClasses.description: Named pages and classes used by the data page. Defaults to an empty stub entry. May contain populated entries for complex activity parameter passing scenarios.

pyPostActivity.description: Activity to run after the data page loads. Empty = none.

pyPassCurrentParamPageForPostActivity.description: Pass the data page parameter page to the post-load activity.

pyLoadActivity.description: Top-level load activity. Empty when using pyDataSourceList (standard pattern).

pyLoadActivityParameters.description: Parameters for the top-level load activity. Usually empty when using pyDataSourceList.

pyLoadReportDefinition.description: Report definition name for report-sourced data pages.

pyKeysForPageList.description: Keys for page list key-based retrieval. Each entry must start with '.' and reference a valid property. Only applicable when pyEnableRetrievePageByKey is 'true'.

pyDataPageSaveOptionList.description: Save plan options for savable data pages (pyPageType: 'savable'). All options whose pySaveOptionWhen evaluates to true are executed.

pyMethodStatus.description: Access classification. 'API' = exposed for external use. 'Internal' = platform-only. Empty = unclassified.

then.x-pega-condition: pyEnableRetrievePageByKey must be 'false' or empty when pyStructure is 'page' (server forces this)
savable page type drives pyType='loadonly' and pyIsSavable='true'
normal page type drives pyType='normal', pyIsSavable='false', pyUseNewGenPageForEditableDataPage='false'
loadonly page type drives pyType='loadonly' and pyIsSavable='false'

MethodParam.description: A data page parameter entry (Embed-MethodParams). Parameters become part of the cache key.

pyParametersParamName.description: Parameter name (e.g., 'postalCode', 'customerId'). The name 'pyImplicitParameter' is reserved and cannot be used.

pyParametersParamReq.description: Required flag. '-1' or 'Y' = required, '0' or 'N' = optional.

pyParametersParamDefaultValue.description: Default value if the parameter is not passed by the caller.

pyIsAlternateKeyStorage.description: Marks this parameter as part of an alternate key storage pattern. Used on savable data pages to identify which fields constitute the record key for save/patch/delete operations.

pyAltKeyStorageField.description: Field name used for alternate key storage. Uses dot-prefix notation (e.g., '.pyGUID', '.pxInsName'). Only set when pyIsAlternateKeyStorage is 'true'.

DeclarePageSource.description: A data source entry (Embed-DeclarePageSource). Defines how the data page loads its data — via connector, data transform, activity, report definition, or aggregated sub-sources.

pyDeclarePagesDataSource.description: Source type. 'Connector' = REST/SOAP connector. 'DataTransform' = data transform. 'LoadActivity' = custom activity. 'ObjOpen' = lookup by key. 'ReportDefinition' = report definition. 'AggregateSources' = aggregates multiple sub-sources. 'GenAI' = Generative AI connector (server auto-sets pyLoadActivity to pxCallGenAI). 'KnowledgeBuddy' = Knowledge Buddy query (server auto-sets pyLoadActivity to pxCallKnowledgeBuddy).

pySourceWhen.description: When rule name that controls whether this source is used. 'Always' = unconditional (use for single-source data pages or as fallback in multi-source).

pyStructure.description: Target structure. Typically mirrors the top-level pyStructure.

pyConnectorName.description: Connector rule name (Connector, GenAI, and KnowledgeBuddy sources).

pyConnectorClassName.description: Class the connector is defined on (Connector sources only).

pyConnectorList.description: Connector rule type (Connector sources only). Note: the server auto-fills this as a default value even on non-connector sources — agents should only set this for Connector sources.

pyLoadActivity.description: Load activity name. 'pxCallConnector' for Connector sources (auto-set by server). 'pxCallDataTransform' for DataTransform sources (auto-set by server). 'pxCallRetrieveReportData' for ReportDefinition sources (auto-set by server). Custom activity name for LoadActivity sources.

pyReqDataTransform.description: Request data transform name. Prepares the request before calling the connector or populates the data page directly (DataTransform sources).

pyDTName.description: Data Transform name. Required for DataTransform sources — server validates this field, not pyReqDataTransform.

pyResDataTransform.description: Response data transform name. Maps the connector response to the data page's class structure. Mandatory for Connector sources.

pyPassCurrentParamPageForReqDT.description: Pass the parameter page to the request data transform.

pyPassCurrentParamPageForRespDT.description: Pass the parameter page to the response data transform.

pyPassCurrentParamPageForActivity.description: Pass the parameter page to pxCallConnector or the custom load activity.

pyExecuteOnDataPage.description: Execute the data transform on the data page clipboard vs. the connector page.

pyEditable.description: Editable flag on the source entry.

pyIsSimulated.description: Whether this source entry is a simulation replacement. When 'true', the original pre-simulation source is preserved in pyDisabledSource.

pyIsRequired.description: Whether this source must succeed for the data page to load.

pyDataSourceHolder.description: Display identifier (usually the connector or DT name).

pyModalTitle.description: UI display label for the source entry. Auto-populated from the source's primary identifier (When rule name, connector name, DT name, etc.). Agents should not set this field.

pyRuleOpenerKey.description: Key for opening the connector rule (format: 'CLASSNAME!CONNECTORNAME').

pyEndPointURL.description: Override endpoint URL. Empty = use the connector's default.

pyPageListPropClassName.description: Page list property class. Auto-set to 'Code-Pega-List' for list-structure data pages. Agents should not need to set this.

pyReorderFlag.description: UI tracking field that records whether this source entry was reordered in the Pega editor. Does NOT affect runtime evaluation order — array position in pyDataSourceList determines that. Auto-managed.

pyPassCurrentParamPageForWhen.description: Pass the parameter page to the When rule (multi-source data pages).

pyConnectorParamList.description: Maps data page parameters to connector parameters.

pyConnectorDTParams.description: Parameters for the connector's data transforms. Can contain populated Embed-NameValuePair entries for connector DT parameter mapping (same structure as pyConnectorParamList).

pyWhenParamList.description: Parameters passed to the When rule (multi-source data pages).

pyDisabledSource.description: Preserves the original pre-simulation source verbatim. Only present on simulated data pages. Required by the 'un-simulate' UI workflow — must be preserved on updates. Omit when creating non-simulated data pages.

pyLoadActivityParameters.description: Internal serialization used by pxCallConnector. Mirrors many source fields. Usually auto-populated.

pyDSSystemName.description: System name for the data source. Optional but generates a warning if empty.

pyActivityParams.description: Maps data page parameters to activity parameters. Available on all source types (not just LoadActivity). The activity-source equivalent of pyConnectorParamList for connector sources. Server auto-populates pyLoadActivityParameters from this array.

pyDTParamList.description: Maps data page parameters to data transform parameters (DataTransform sources). The DT-source equivalent of pyConnectorParamList for connector sources and pyActivityParams for activity sources.

pyRunDataTransformOnError.description: When 'true', runs the error data transform if the source load fails. Default: not set (equivalent to 'false').

pyLoadReportDefinition.description: Report definition rule name (ReportDefinition sources only).

pyReportDefinitionClass.description: Class the report definition is defined on (ReportDefinition sources only).

pyLookupClassName.description: Class to look up by key (ObjOpen sources only). E.g., 'Data-Admin-Operator-ID'.

pyLookupName.description: Display name for the lookup (ObjOpen sources only). E.g., 'Operator ID'.

pyClassKeyValueList.description: Database lookup keys for ObjOpen sources. Each entry maps a key property to a value or parameter reference.

pyTemporaryObject.description: Controls temporary page behavior during source loading. Rarely used.

pyRATimeout.description: Timeout in seconds for source load (string-encoded integer, e.g., '30'). Found on ReportDefinition and LoadActivity sources.

pySkipSummaryProcessing.description: Skip summary processing for ReportDefinition sources.

pyAggregatedDataSourceList.description: Sub-sources for AggregateSources type. Each entry is a DeclarePageSource that loads independently; results are aggregated into the parent data page.

pyAggregatedDataSourcesLabel.description: Display label for the aggregated source group (AggregateSources type).

pySourceID.description: Unique source identifier within the data page. Used for hierarchical source identification in AggregateSources.

pyParentSourceID.description: Parent source identifier. Links a sub-source to its parent AggregateSources entry.

pyKBName.description: Knowledge Buddy rule name. Required for KnowledgeBuddy sources.

pyKBQuery.description: Query text for Knowledge Buddy. Typically 'Param.<Name>' to wire from a data page parameter.

pyKBQueryJSON.description: Structured JSON query for Knowledge Buddy. Alternative to pyKBQuery for complex queries.

pyKBResponse.description: Dot-prefixed property reference where the Knowledge Buddy response is stored (e.g., '.pyResponseData').

then.x-pega-condition: Connector source requires pyConnectorName, pyConnectorClassName, and pyConnectorList
LoadActivity source requires pyLoadActivity and pyPassCurrentParamPageForActivity
ReportDefinition source requires pyLoadReportDefinition, pyReportDefinitionClass, and pyPassCurrentParamPageForActivity
ObjOpen source requires pyLookupClassName and pyClassKeyValueList

DisabledSource.description: Represents an alternative source type that was not chosen (Embed-DeclarePageSource with minimal fields).

pyDescription.description: Display description of the alternative source.

NameValuePair.description: A name-value pair entry (Embed-NameValuePair). Used for connector parameter mapping, DT params, When params, and post-activity params.

pyIsActivityParameter.description: Set to 'false' for connector parameters (query string, path, header — placed on the connector's parameter page where pyMapFrom: 'PARAM' reads them). Set to 'true' only for pxCallConnector activity-level parameters (e.g., timeout overrides). Using 'true' for connector parameters causes values to be silently dropped — the connector receives no parameter values.

SaveOption.description: A save plan option entry (Embed-DeclarePageSaveOption). Defines how a savable data page persists changes back to the source.

pySaveOptionType.description: Save mechanism. 'simplesave' = standard database save (most common). 'activity' = custom save activity. 'dbDelete' = database delete. 'simplepatch' = database patch/update. 'connector' = REST/SOAP connector-based save (OAS-imported).

pySaveOptionWhen.description: When rule name that controls whether this save option executes. 'Always' = unconditional. When conditional, Pega evaluates save options in order.

pySaveOptionClassName.description: Save option class name — usually mirrors pyClassName. May be empty on activity type.

pySaveOptionClassLabel.description: Human-readable label for the save option class.

pyDescription.description: Auto-generated description of the save option.

pyStructure.description: Structure — matches the data page structure.

pyConnectorType.description: Connector rule type. Server sets 'Rule-Connect-REST' even for DB saves.

pyRESTMethod.description: HTTP method. Server sets 'POST' for DB saves.

pyDTName.description: Data transform for the save operation.

pyValidateRule.description: Validation When rule to run before saving.

pyActivityName.description: Custom save activity name (activity type only).

pyRATimeout.description: Timeout in seconds for the save activity (e.g., '30'). Activity type only.

pyActivityParamList.description: Parameters for the save activity (activity type only).

pyWhenParamList.description: Parameters passed to the pySaveOptionWhen rule (conditional saves only).

pyDTParamList.description: Data transform parameters for save-time DT execution. Same structure as pyDTParamList on source entries.

pyRuleOpenerKey.description: Key for opening the connector rule.

pyIsRequired.description: Whether the save option is required.

pyIsResponseDTRequired.description: Whether a response data transform is required.

pyReadsOrRoutesToExternalSystem.description: Whether this save routes to an external system.

pyIsSaveOptionDeleted.description: Marks a save option as soft-deleted (still in array but inactive). Auto-managed.

pyReorderFlag.description: Indicates save option ordering has been customized. Auto-managed.

pyModalTitle.description: Title for modal dialog shown during save (if applicable). Auto-managed.
